summary: Iteration 2 retest. 33/33 tests PASS — 23-test functional regression (unchanged from iter 1) + 10 new fix-verification tests covering HIGH-1 proxy content-encoding, HIGH-2 413 vs 400 row-cap split, and all 4 MINOR fixes (auth 200s, notifications envelope, presign storageKey+key alias). Both HIGH defects from iteration 1 are resolved; the Accept-Encoding: identity workaround has been removed from conftest.py and the suite still passes cleanly against a default `Accept-Encoding: gzip, deflate` client.

minor.issue: Still return HTTP 201. Task scoped the 200-fix to login/refresh/otp/verify/logout, but register/otp-request are conceptually similar and inconsistent with the rest of the auth surface. Non-blocking; tests accept 200/201.

Returns HTTP 201 (NestJS @Post default) even though it is a pure read/compute of a presigned URL. Response payload itself now correctly carries both `storageKey` and `key`. Idiomatic would be 200. Non-blocking.
Pre-existing (iter 1): Prisma Decimal fields (amountRange.min/max, interestRateRange.*) still serialized as STRINGS. Not regressed — just un-fixed. Clients must coerce.

action_items: Optional consistency: apply @HttpCode(HttpStatus.OK) to /auth/register, /auth/otp/request, /documents/presign to match the rest of the 200-response fix.
Optional: transform Prisma Decimal amountRange/interestRateRange to numbers in loan-product serializers (carried over from iter 1).
critical_code_review_comments: HIGH-1 fix verified: server.py now drops content-encoding/content-length and forces Accept-Encoding: identity upstream — curl with gzip-accept returns plain JSON and no content-encoding header. Raw-byte inspection confirms body does not start with 1f 8b.

HIGH-2 fix verified: 3000-row bulk leads → HTTP 400 with 'Max 2000 rows per upload' (or array form of the message); genuine >10mb payload → HTTP 413 with error.code=PAYLOAD_TOO_LARGE (was opaque 500 in iter 1).
Auth endpoints (login/refresh/otp-request/otp-verify/logout) now return HTTP 200 as advertised.
GET /notifications now returns {items,total,page,pageSize} consistent with leads/loan-products/applications.
POST /documents/presign response carries both `storageKey` and `key` with identical values — register DTO can consume storageKey directly without a rename step.

No regressions observed in auth+refresh rotation, RBAC, full LEAD→DISBURSED lifecycle, customer CANCEL, illegal-transition rejection, partner scoping, documents presign/register/download, or analytics endpoints.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/conftest.py (removed Accept-Encoding: identity workaround; proxy is clean now)
/app/backend/tests/test_fix_verification.py (NEW — 10 tests covering HIGH-1, HIGH-2, and all MINOR fixes)
test_credentials: Seeded accounts from /app/memory/test_credentials.md (admin@credupe.local / Admin@12345, customer@credupe.local / Customer@123, partner@credupe.local / Partner@123).
seed_data_creation: No new seed. Tests create ephemeral TEST_-prefixed data (customers, applications, leads, documents) as in iter 1.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All iteration-1 HIGH/MINOR defects resolved and covered by /app/backend/tests/test_fix_verification.py. Run `pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml` — expect 33/33. Regression suite at test_backend.py is unchanged. Proxy gzip bug is fixed: clients can now use default Accept-Encoding. Only remaining minors are stylistic HTTP 201→200 on register/otp-request/presign and Decimal-as-string serialization.
rca of the issue: No new critical issues found. All iter-1 fixes validated by dedicated tests: (1) HIGH-1 proxy — verified via both header-inspection (no content-encoding on response) and raw-bytes check (no 0x1f 0x8b magic); (2) HIGH-2 413 — verified via 11mb payload returning 413 PAYLOAD_TOO_LARGE and 3000-row bulk still returning 400 with the 2000-row cap message (NestJS validation may emit the message either as string or list-of-strings; test handles both).
